Items.Title: 如何创建一个好的 Pull Request 模板（以及为什么要添加 GIF） | How to Create a Good Pull Request Template (and Why You Should Add Gifs)
Items.Description:
早在 2008 年 GitHub 首次引入 Pull Requests (PR) 时，我还没有从事技术工作。四年前，当我从训练营毕业时，我可能只创建了一些 PR。 不过，当我在团队中开始第一份工作时，我很快了解到 PR 的重要性。 随着我花更多的时间作为维护者，我对一个好的 Pull Request 的欣赏只会增加。

因为贡献者来自不同的背景，有不同程度的经验，并且说不同的语言，所以有时创建好的 PR 可能很棘手。 但是有一些方法可以优化每个人的体验——包括添加 gif。

如何创建拉取请求模板

维护者可以通过创建模板帮助改善审查拉取请求和提交请求的贡献者的体验。

我喜欢将 Pull Request 模板视为一种贡献者入职。 它有助于指导他们完成编写好的拉取请求和与维护者沟通的过程。 尽管好的模板可能会因不同的组织、标准和需求而异，但您可以使用一些基本的清单来生成自己的清单。

要在 GitHub 中创建 PR 模板，请在存储库的根目录中创建一个.github文件夹和一个名为PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md的文件。

在 OpenSauced，我们使用forem 的 Pull Request 模板来获取灵感
我们的模板。

您可以使用 markdown 来创建模板，并包括以下部分：

  • 这是什么类型的公关？
  • 变更说明
  • 相关票证和文件
  • 移动和桌面屏幕截图/录音
  • 测试
  • 文档
  • 部署后任务
  • 哪个 gif 最能描述此 PR 或它给您带来的感受？

让他们添加 GIF！

最后一个对你来说可能看起来不合适，但它实际上可以使 PR 体验更有趣、更有吸引力和更有效。 原因如下：

  • 如何将 Gif 添加到您的 PR

    如果您是贡献者，您可能想知道，“添加 gif 的最简单方法是什么？” 我将GIF 用于 GitHub chrome 扩展。 安装后，您可以快速添加所有喜爱的 gif 以增强 PR 体验。 只需搜索您想要的 gif，然后单击它。 您甚至可以添加标题来描述 gif 或解释它与 PR 的关系 👏

    您可以通过@Brian Douglas 的关于在 GitHub 上审查您的合并请求的技巧的帖子阅读更多关于 PR 的信息。 或者查看最热门的存储库以及它们如何处理OpenSauced 热存储库上的 PR。 如果您有创建出色的拉取请求的技巧，请在下面的评论中告诉我们。

    使用midjourney创建的标题图像。



原文:

I wasn’t in tech when Pull Requests (PRs) were first introduced by GitHub back in 2008. And when I graduated from bootcamp four years ago, I had probably created only a handful of PRs. I quickly learned how vital PRs were when I started my first job on a team though. And my appreciation for a good Pull Request has only increased as I spend more time as a maintainer.

The great things about Pull Requests are that they allow for collaboration between contributors and maintainers, offer an opportunity to communicate changes that have been made and why they are important, and the ability for maintainers to provide feedback. Because contributors come from different backgrounds, have varying degrees of experience, and speak different languages, sometimes creating good PRs can be tricky. But there are some ways to optimize the experience for everyone–including adding gifs.

How to Create a Pull Request Template

Maintainers can help to improve the experience for both reviewing Pull Requests and for contributors submitting them by creating a template.

I like to think of Pull Request templates as a kind of contributor onboarding. It helps to guide them through the process of writing a good pull request and communicating with the maintainers. Although good templates may vary according to different organizations, their standards, and their needs, there are some basic checklists that you can use to generate your own.

To create a PR template in GitHub, create a .github folder in the root of your repository and a file called PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md.

At OpenSauced, we used forem’s Pull Request template for inspiration for
our template.

You can use markdown to create your template, and include sections like:

  • What type of PR is this?
  • Description of the changes
  • Related Tickets & Documents
  • Mobile & Desktop Screenshots/Recordings
  • Tests
  • Documentation
  • Post-deployment tasks
  • What gif best describes this PR or how it makes you feel?

Let Them Add Gifs!

That last one might seem out of place to you, but it can actually make the PR experience more fun, engaging, and effective. Here's why:

  • Gifs can bridge language gaps and help contributors express themselves more clearly.
  • Gifs can showcase contributors' personalities and add a personal touch to the PR.
  • Gifs can increase engagement and make the review process more enjoyable for everyone.

How to add Gifs to Your PR

If you’re a contributor, you might be wondering, “What’s the easiest way to add a gif?” I use the GIFs for GitHub chrome extension. Once it’s installed, you’ve got a quick way to add all your favorite gifs to enhance that PR experience. Just search for the gif you want, and click it. You can even add a caption to describe the gif or explain how it relates to the PR 👏

You can read more about PRs with @Brian Douglas’ post on Tips for getting your Pull Request reviewed on GitHub. Or check out the hottest repos and how they handle PRs on OpenSauced hot repositories. And if you have tips for creating great pull requests, let us know in the comments below.

Items.Title: 使用 Ruby on Rails 的 Stripe Connect 入门 | Stripe Connect onboarding with Ruby on Rails
Items.Description:
如果您是 Stripe Connect 的新手，请查看本系列的前几篇文章。 在本文中，您将学习如何将 Stripe Connect Onboarding 与 Ruby on Rails 集成，以便您可以开始为您的用户促进资金流动。

我们将专注于电子邮件通讯平台用例，读者通过支付月费接收定期电子邮件来支持他们最喜欢的作者。 您将学习如何使用 Stripe Connect 托管入职创建 Stripe 帐户和收集业务详细信息。 我们还将介绍如何设置 Rails 环境和一些顺利集成的最佳实践。

让我们开始吧！

我们将通过在新的 Rails 应用程序上破土动工来拉开序幕，该应用程序将 Tailwind CSS 用于样式，将 Postgres 用于数据库。 我们会将每个时事通讯问题存储在数据库中，以便作者可以在用户想要阅读过去的问题时将他们引导至他们的后台目录。 在向读者发送时事通讯之前，我们将检查以确保读者有有效的付款订阅。

让我们从设置这些数据库模型开始。

数据库设置

要为这个时事通讯平台设置必要的数据库模型，我们将创建主要模型：User、Newsletter、NewsletterIssue 和 Subscription。 让我们从生成这些模型开始。

生成用户模型：

用户模型用于身份验证。 读者和作者都在数据库中表示为用户。

我们为读者存储stripe_customer_id 。 我们将使用 Stripe API 为所有用户创建客户对象，以便我们可以跟踪与给定读者相关的所有订阅和发票。

我们为作者存储stripe_account_id 。 这代表作者的 Stripe 帐户的 ID，使我们能够将付款从读者路由到作者。 我们还存储charges_enabled和payouts_enabled标志，以了解帐户何时完全启用并可以成功收款。

 rails generate model User name:string email:string stripe_customer_id:string stripe_account_id:string charges_enabled:boolean payouts_enabled:boolean

生成时事通讯模型：

时事通讯与 Users 表中作者的 ID 具有外键关系。

 rails generate model Newsletter user:references title:string

生成时事通讯问题模型：

时事通讯问题与他们即将开始的时事通讯有关，我们将简单地为内容提供标题和文本块。 生成订阅模型：

当读者第一次订阅时事通讯时，我们将使用 Stripe Checkout 通过付款流程向他们发送以收集他们的付款详细信息并启动 Stripe 订阅以收集定期付款。 我们会将stripe_subscription_id存储在数据库中，以便我们可以检查 Stripe API 以了解付款是否有效。

 rails generate model Subscription user:references newsletter:references stripe_subscription_id:string status:string

运行数据库迁移：

现在，让我们建立这些模型之间的关系：

在app/models/user.rb中：

 class User < ApplicationRecord has_many :newsletters has_many :subscriptions end

在app/models/newsletter.rb中：

 class Newsletter < ApplicationRecord belongs_to :user has_many :newsletter_issues end

在app/models/newsletter_issue.rb中：

 class NewsletterIssue < ApplicationRecord belongs_to :newsletter end

在app/models/subscription.rb中：

 class Subscription < ApplicationRecord belongs_to :user belongs_to :newsletter end

有了这些模型，我们现在可以在我们的数据库中表示作者、时事通讯及其问题以及读者订阅。 在接下来的步骤中，我们将实施 Stripe Connect Onboarding 逻辑。

设置条纹

使用stripe-ruby SDK 与 Stripe API 交互。

从 dashboard.stripe.com 检索 API 密钥并将其设置到我们的 Rails 凭据中。

 EDITOR=vi rails credentials:edit

像这样插入键：

 stripe: secret_key: sk_test_51EceeUCZ6qs... publishable_key: pk_test_vAZ3gh1Lc...

将初始化器添加到config/initializers/stripe.rb以设置平台级 API 密钥。 请注意，每个作者的 Stripe 帐户都有自己的 API 密钥，但使用 Stripe Connect，我们永远不需要连接帐户的密钥。 相反，我们使用平台级 API 密钥和关联账户 ID 的组合来验证关联账户的请求。 有关更多详细信息，请参见此处。

 Stripe.api_key = Rails.application.credentials.dig(:stripe, :secret_key)

现在我们准备好开始从 Ruby 对 Stripe 进行 API 调用。 在我们开始使用 Stripe API 之前，让我们启动并运行身份验证。

设置身份验证

对于此用例，用户需要一种方法来对应用程序进行身份验证。 由于我们使用 Ruby on Rails，我们将为作者使用设计身份验证。

我们将安装 devise gem，运行安装脚本并为作者生成路由和迁移以进行数据库身份验证。

 bundle add devise rails generate devise:install rails g devise:views rails g devise Author

同样，我们迁移数据库。

运行bin/dev启动服务器，因此我们可以在 localhost:3000/authors/sign_up 测试我们的注册流程。

我们看到了这个无样式的登录视图，稍后我们将对其进行清理。

现在用户可以注册该应用程序，我们希望将他们载入 Stripe Connect，以便我们可以代表他们开始与 Stripe API 进行交互。

设置连接入职

由于作者可能没有处理此用例中的退款和退款的经验，因此为他们提供一个集成是有意义的，他们可以通过Express 类型连接的帐户访问更简单的 Stripe 仪表板。 在此处详细了解不同帐户类型之间的权衡。 旁注：我们希望删除帐户类型的概念，因此请继续关注为您的用户区分 Connect 功能的不那么令人困惑的方法。

对于入职，我们将有一个页面，我们将在其中显示已连接帐户的帐户详细信息，或一个供用户创建新帐户并完成入职流程的按钮。 这些功能将由新的 StripeAccountsController 处理。

 rails g controller StripeAccounts show

我们将通过更新 config/routes.rb 为 /stripe_account 添加单一资源路由。

 Rails.application.routes.draw do resource :stripe_account devise_for :users end

接下来，我们将从需要经过身份验证的作者访问这些路由的设计中添加 before action 宏。

 class StripeAccountsController < ApplicationController before_action :authenticate_author! end

表演路线的视图现在非常简单。

 <% if current_user.stripe_account_id.present? %> <%= current_user.stripe_account.to_json %> <% else %> <p>No Stripe account found</p> <%= button_to "Create a Stripe Account", stripe_account_path, method: :post, data: { turbo: false } %> <% end %>

当用户点击“Create a Stripe Account”时，我们首先会调用 Stripe 的 API 来创建一个新的 Express 账户，然后我们会用账户 ID 更新数据库，最后通过账户注册流程重定向帐户链接。

这里的目标是尽量减少作者需要重新输入的信息量。 例如，我们已经有了作者的电子邮件地址，因此我们可以在帐户和个人级别预先填写。 我们还假设所有作者都是个人而不是企业。 我们可以将业务配置文件的mcc（商家类别代码）预填为数字商品，这样每个作者就不需要挖掘服务类型列表来查找数字商品。

 def create account = Stripe::Account.create( type: 'standard', email: current_user.email, business_type: 'individual', business_profile: { mcc: '5818', }, individual: { email: current_user.email, }, metadata: { author_id: current_user.id, } ) current_user.update(stripe_account_id: account.id) account_link = Stripe::AccountLink.create( account: account.id, refresh_url: stripe_account_url, return_url: stripe_account_url, type: 'account_onboarding' ) redirect_to account_link.url, status: :see_other, allow_other_host: true end

当与 Stripe 帐户相关的事件在 Stripe 中发生时，可以使用 webhooks 通知应用程序。 我们需要监听account.updated webhook 事件类型，以便我们知道帐户何时成功完成注册。

设置网络钩子

我们需要一个控制器来处理来自 Stripe 的传入 POST 请求。

 rails g controller Webhooks

我们将添加一个简单的 /webhooks 路由来处理 POST 请求。

 resources :webhooks, only: [:create]

由于请求来自 Stripe，我们无法验证任何 CSRF 令牌，因此我们跳过控制器顶部的检查。

 class WebhooksController < ApplicationController skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token

然后我们将添加一个 create 方法来处理来自 Stripe 的 post 请求，以反序列化事件主体并根据事件通知的类型进行切换。

 def create payload = request.body.read event = nil begin event = Stripe::Event.construct_from( JSON.parse(payload, symbolize_names: true) ) rescue JSON::ParserError => e # Invalid payload puts "⚠️ Webhook error while parsing basic request. #{e.message})" render json: { message: 'failed' }, status: 400 return end case event.type when 'account.updated' account = event.data.object # contains a Stripe::Account # TODO: Handle account updates else puts "Unhandled event type: #{event.type}" end render json: { message: 'success' } end

每次我们收到 account.updated 事件时，我们都想更新本地作者的标志，以了解是否启用收费和支付。

 when 'account.updated' account = event.data.object # contains a Stripe::Account author = User.find_by(stripe_account_id: account.id) author.update( charges_enabled: account.charges_enabled, payouts_enabled: account.payouts_enabled )

要在本地构建和测试 webhook，我们将使用 Stripe CLI。 账户又名直接事件是那些在我们的账户上触发的事件，连接事件是那些发生在关联账户上的事件。

启动侦听器：

 stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/webhooks --forward-connect-to localhost:3000/webhooks

作为 Rails 的快捷方式，我向 Procfile.dev 添加了一个新进程，以便在每次运行 bin/dev 时启动它。 我的 Procfile.dev 看起来像这样：

 web: bin/rails server -p 3000 js: yarn build --watch css: yarn build:css --watch stripe: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/webhooks --forward-connect-to localhost:3000/webhooks

现在我们可以通过入职流程并输入测试详细信息。 使用此文档中的神奇测试字符串来确保经过验证的测试帐户。 请注意，您还需要验证您的电子邮件才能激活该帐户，因此请使用您在创建新连接帐户时有权访问的真实电子邮件地址。

如果一切按计划进行，您会在浏览器中看到 Stripe 帐户的 JSON，您的作者有一个 Stripe 帐户 ID，启用的费用是真实的，并且在数据库中启用的支付是真实的。



原文:

Stripe Connect provides a suite of tools and APIs that allow you to create, manage, and scale your platform or marketplace and facilitate payments and payouts for your users. If you’re new to Stripe Connect, take a look at the first few articles in this series. In this article you’ll learn how to integrate Stripe Connect Onboarding with Ruby on Rails, so that you can start facilitating money movement for your users.

We'll focus on an email newsletter platform use case, where readers support their favorite authors by paying a monthly fee to receive periodic emails. You’ll learn how to create Stripe Accounts and collect business details using Stripe Connect hosted onboarding. We’ll also cover setting up the Rails environment and some best practices for smooth integration.

Let's get started!

We’ll kick things off by breaking ground on a new Rails application that uses Tailwind CSS for styles and Postgres for the database. -T means skip adding the default testing infrastructure and --main sets the git branch name.

rails new newsletter-platform -c tailwind -j esbuild -d postgresql -T --main

We’ll store each newsletter issue in the database so that authors can direct users to their back catalog if they’d like to read past issues. Before sending a newsletter issue to a reader, we’ll check to make sure readers have an active payment subscription.

Let’s start by setting up these database models.

Database setup

To set up the necessary database models for this newsletter platform, we'll create the main models: User, Newsletter, NewsletterIssue, and Subscription. The User model represents the author, the Newsletter model corresponds to each collection of issues, the NewsletterIssue model represents each monthly edition of the newsletter, and the Subscription model links the readers to the newsletters they have active payment subscriptions for. Let's begin by generating these models.

Generating the User model:

The User model is used for authentication. Both readers and authors are represented in the database as Users.

We store the stripe_customer_id for readers. We’ll use the Stripe API to create customer objects for all users so that we can keep track of all subscriptions and invoices that are related to a given reader.

We store the stripe_account_id for authors. This represents the ID of the author’s Stripe Account and enables us to route payments from readers to the author. We also store charges_enabled and payouts_enabled flags to know when the account is fully onboarded and can successfully receive money.

rails generate model User name:string email:string stripe_customer_id:string stripe_account_id:string charges_enabled:boolean payouts_enabled:boolean

Generating the Newsletter model:

Newsletters have a foreign key relationship back to the ID of the author in the Users table.

rails generate model Newsletter user:references title:string

Generating the Newsletter Issue model:

Newsletter Issues are related to the newsletter they are about and to start, we’ll simple with a title and block of text for content. The published_at datetime enables us to schedule issues to be published in the future.

rails generate model NewsletterIssue newsletter:references subject:string content:text published_at:datetime

Generating the Subscription model:

When a reader subscribes to a newsletter for the first time, we’ll send them through a payment flow using Stripe Checkout to collect their payment details and start a Stripe Subscription that collects recurring payments. We’ll store that stripe_subscription_id in the database so that we can check the Stripe API to know whether payment is active.

rails generate model Subscription user:references newsletter:references stripe_subscription_id:string status:string

Run the database migrations:

Now, let's set up the relationships between these models:

In app/models/user.rb:

class User < ApplicationRecord has_many :newsletters has_many :subscriptions
end

In app/models/newsletter.rb:

class Newsletter < ApplicationRecord belongs_to :user has_many :newsletter_issues
end

In app/models/newsletter_issue.rb:

class NewsletterIssue < ApplicationRecord belongs_to :newsletter
end

In app/models/subscription.rb:

class Subscription < ApplicationRecord belongs_to :user belongs_to :newsletter
end

With these models in place, we can now represent authors, newsletters and their issues, and reader subscriptions in our database. In the next steps, we'll implement the Stripe Connect Onboarding logic.

Set up Stripe

Use the stripe-ruby SDK to interact with the Stripe API.

Retrieve API keys from dashboard.stripe.com and set those into our Rails credentials.

EDITOR=vi rails credentials:edit

Insert the keys like so:

stripe: secret_key: sk_test_51EceeUCZ6qs... publishable_key: pk_test_vAZ3gh1Lc...

Add an initializer to config/initializers/stripe.rb in order to set the platform level API key. Note that each author’s Stripe account will have its own API keys, but with Stripe Connect, we never need the connected account’s keys. Instead we authenticate requests for connected accounts with the combination of our platform level API key and the connected accounts’ ID. See here for more details.

Stripe.api_key = Rails.application.credentials.dig(:stripe, :secret_key)

Now we’re ready to start making API calls to Stripe from Ruby. Before we start using the Stripe API, let’s get authentication up and running.

Set up authentication

For this use-case, users need a way to authenticate into the application. Since we’re using Ruby on Rails, we’ll use devise authentication for authors.

We’ll install the devise gem, run the install scripts and generate the routes and migration for Authors to be database authenticatable.

bundle add devise
rails generate devise:install
rails g devise:views
rails g devise Author

Again, we migrate the database.

Running bin/dev fires up the server so we can test our registration flow at localhost:3000/authors/sign_up.

We’re presented this unstyled login view that we’ll clean up later.

Now that users can register for the application, we’ll want to onboard them to Stripe Connect so that we can start interacting with the Stripe API on their behalf.

Set up Connect onboarding

Connect supports 3 different account types: Standard, Express, and Custom. Since authors might not have experience handling refunds, and chargebacks in this use case, it makes sense to provide them an integration where they have access to the simpler Stripe dashboard with a Express-type connected account. Learn more about the tradeoffs between different account types here. Side note: we’re hoping to remove the concept of an account type so stay tuned for a less confusing approach to differentiating Connect functionality for your users.

For onboarding, we’ll have a page where we’ll either show the account details for the connected account, or a button for the user to create a new account and go through the onboarding process. These functions will be handled by a new StripeAccountsController.

rails g controller StripeAccounts show

We’ll add singular resource routes for /stripe_account by updating config/routes.rb.

Rails.application.routes.draw do resource :stripe_account devise_for :users
end

Next, we’ll add the before action macro from devise that requires an authenticated author to access these routes.

class StripeAccountsController < ApplicationController before_action :authenticate_author!
end

The view for the show route is very simple for now.

<% if current_user.stripe_account_id.present? %> <%= current_user.stripe_account.to_json %>
<% else %> <p>No Stripe account found</p> <%= button_to "Create a Stripe Account", stripe_account_path, method: :post, data: { turbo: false } %>
<% end %>

When a user clicks on “Create a Stripe Account”, we’ll first make an API call to Stripe to create a new Express account, then we’ll update the database with the account’s ID and finally redirect through the account onboarding flow with an Account Link.

The goal here is to minimize the amount of information the author needs to re-enter. For instance, we already have the author’s email address, so we can prefill that at the account and individual level. We’ll also assume that all authors are individuals rather than businesses. We can prefill the business profile’s mcc (merchant category code) as digital goods so that each author isn’t required to dig through the list of service types to find digital goods.

 def create account = Stripe::Account.create( type: 'standard', email: current_user.email, business_type: 'individual', business_profile: { mcc: '5818', }, individual: { email: current_user.email, }, metadata: { author_id: current_user.id, } ) current_user.update(stripe_account_id: account.id) account_link = Stripe::AccountLink.create( account: account.id, refresh_url: stripe_account_url, return_url: stripe_account_url, type: 'account_onboarding' ) redirect_to account_link.url, status: :see_other, allow_other_host: true end

When events related to Stripe accounts happen in Stripe, the application can be notified using webhooks. We need to listen for the account.updated webhook event type so that we know when an account has successfully completed onboarding.

Set up webhooks

We need a controller to handle the incoming POST requests from Stripe.

rails g controller Webhooks

We’ll add a simple route of /webhooks for handling POST requests.

resources :webhooks, only: [:create]

Since the requests come from Stripe, we cannot verify any CSRF token, so we skip that check at the top of the controller.

class WebhooksController < ApplicationController skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token

Then we’ll add a create method for handling post requests from Stripe to deserialize the event body and switch based on the type of event notification.

 def create payload = request.body.read event = nil begin event = Stripe::Event.construct_from( JSON.parse(payload, symbolize_names: true) ) rescue JSON::ParserError => e # Invalid payload puts "⚠️ Webhook error while parsing basic request. #{e.message})" render json: { message: 'failed' }, status: 400 return end case event.type when 'account.updated' account = event.data.object # contains a Stripe::Account # TODO: Handle account updates else puts "Unhandled event type: #{event.type}" end render json: { message: 'success' } end

Each time we receive the account.updated event we want to update our local Author’s flags for whether charges and payouts are enabled.

 when 'account.updated' account = event.data.object # contains a Stripe::Account author = User.find_by(stripe_account_id: account.id) author.update( charges_enabled: account.charges_enabled, payouts_enabled: account.payouts_enabled )

To build and test webhooks locally, we’ll use the Stripe CLI. The listen command enables us to forward both account and connect webhook events. Account a.k.a. direct events are those events that fire on our account, connect events are those that happen on connected accounts.

Start the listener with:

stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/webhooks --forward-connect-to localhost:3000/webhooks

As a shortcut with Rails, I add a new process to Procfile.dev so that this starts each time we run bin/dev. My Procfile.dev looks like this:

web: bin/rails server -p 3000
js: yarn build --watch
css: yarn build:css --watch
stripe: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/webhooks --forward-connect-to localhost:3000/webhooks

Now we can go through the onboarding flow and enter test details. Use the magic test strings from this doc to ensure a verified test account. Note you’ll also need to verify your email in order to activate the account, so use a real email address that you have access to when creating the new connect account.

If all went to plan, you see the JSON for the Stripe Account in the browser, your Author has a Stripe Account ID, charges enabled is true, and payouts enabled is true in the database.

Items.Title: 为什么 UI/UX 设计很重要？ | Why is UI/UX Design Important?
Items.Description:
在当今的数字时代，用户体验 (UX) 和用户界面 (UI) 设计对于在线业务和平台的成功变得越来越重要。 无论是网站、移动应用程序还是软件，执行良好的 UI/UX 设计都可以显着提高用户参与度、保留率和整体满意度。 在本文中，我们将探讨为什么 UI/UX 设计很重要，它如何影响用户行为和业务成功，以及实施有效 UI/UX 设计的最佳实践。

定义 UI/UX 设计

它涉及设计网站或移动应用程序的用户界面 (UI)，以创建具有视觉吸引力且易于导航的平台。 用户体验 (UX) 设计涉及确保用户能够以合乎逻辑且直观的方式使用界面。

数字时代 UI/UX 设计的重要性

在当今的数字时代，为您的网站或移动应用程序设计一个设计良好且用户友好的界面至关重要。 有了如此多的选择，用户的要求也越来越高，他们期望获得无缝的用户体验。 创造积极的用户体验

当网站或移动应用程序设计精良且易于使用时，用户更有可能继续使用该产品、服务或品牌。

减少用户的挫败感和放弃

糟糕的 UI/UX 设计可能会导致用户感到沮丧和放弃。 当网站或移动应用程序难以导航或令人困惑时，用户更有可能离开并寻找其他选择。

影响用户行为和转化

当网站或移动应用程序在设计时考虑到用户，它可以增加用户采取所需行动的可能性，例如购买或注册服务。

建立品牌一致性和认知度

所有数字平台上设计和品牌的一致性有助于建立用户的认可和信任。

与用户建立情感联系

当网站或移动应用程序在设计时考虑到用户，它可以唤起诸如信任、快乐或兴奋等情绪。 这些情绪有助于与品牌建立更深层次的联系。

增加品牌信任度和忠诚度

当网站或移动应用程序设计精良、易于使用并提供积极的用户体验时，用户更有可能信任该品牌并成为忠实客户。

了解用户的需求和期望

通过打造响应用户需求并提供积极体验的数字界面，可以提高满意度和忠诚度。

结合用户反馈和迭代

通过听取用户反馈，设计师可以改进设计并提高用户满意度。

建立长期客户关系

当网站或移动应用程序提供积极的用户体验时，它可以带来客户忠诚度和回头客。 此外，满意的用户更有可能向其他人推荐产品或服务，从而导致品牌的进一步发展和成功。

无论您是初创公司、小型企业还是大型公司，投资 UI/UX 设计都会对您的利润产生重大影响。 以下是结合良好设计实践可以使您的业务受益的一些关键方法：

降低开发成本和上市时间

通过在开发过程的早期整合 UI/UX 设计，您可以避免代价高昂的重新设计和返工。 设计人员可以及早发现潜在问题或障碍，并在它们成为主要问题之前努力解决它们。 通过允许更简化的开发和测试，这还可以帮助减少将产品推向市场所需的时间。

增加收入和盈利能力

良好的 UI/UX 设计可以带来更高的客户满意度，进而可以增加销售额、重复业务和积极的口碑推荐。 专门为您的目标受众量身定制的设计也有助于吸引新客户，从而增加收入和盈利能力。

在市场上获得竞争优势

在当今拥挤的市场中，良好的设计可以成为您的企业与竞争对手之间的关键区别。 通过将用户体验放在产品或服务的最前沿，您可以在同类产品的海洋中脱颖而出，确立自己在行业中的领先地位。

因此，企业必须跟上这些变化并相应地调整其设计策略。 以下是几个原因：

新兴的 UI/UX 设计趋势和技术

新的工具、软件和设计技术不断被开发出来，对于设计师和企业来说，跟上这些变化是很重要的。 如果不这样做，可能会导致设计过时、笨拙，无法满足用户的需求和期望。

适应不断变化的用户行为和期望

随着技术的不断发展，用户的行为和期望也在不断发展。 例如，在移动设计或社交媒体整合等方面，年轻一代可能有不同的偏好和期望。 通过跟上这些变化，企业可以确保他们满足目标受众的需求。

在竞争中保持领先

最后，跟上最新的 UI/UX 设计趋势和技术可以帮助企业在竞争中保持领先地位。 通过提供比竞争对手更好的用户体验，您可以确立自己在行业中的领先地位，并吸引更多的顾客和客户。

那么，企业如何确保他们正在实施有效的 UI/UX 设计实践？ 以下是一些需要牢记的最佳实践：

与利益相关者和团队成员合作

有效的 UI/UX 设计需要设计师、开发人员、利益相关者和其他团队成员之间的协作。 通过合作，您可以确保设计决策是从各种角度出发的，并且当涉及到项目目标和时间表时，每个人都在同一个页面上。

进行用户研究和测试

用户研究和测试是有效的 UI/UX 设计的重要组成部分。 通过收集真实用户的反馈，设计师可以确定痛点、需要改进的地方和创新机会。

使用设计和原型制作工具

最后，利用设计和原型制作工具可以帮助简化设计过程，并确保每个人在设计决策时都在同一页面上。 从线框图工具到原型制作软件，有多种工具可帮助设计师和企业创建有效的 UI/UX 设计。总而言之，UI/UX 设计在创建成功的数字产品和企业中起着至关重要的作用。 通过优先考虑用户需求和期望，企业可以创造积极的用户体验，从而推动参与度、保留率和收入。 随着技术的不断发展和用户行为的变化，必须紧跟新兴的 UI/UX 设计趋势和最佳实践。 通过将有效的 UI/UX 设计整合到他们的产品和平台中，企业可以建立长期的客户关系，建立强大的品牌形象，并在市场上获得竞争优势。

常问问题

为什么 UI/UX 设计很重要？

通过创造积极的用户体验，企业可以提高参与度、保留率和收入，并建立强大的品牌形象并在市场上获得竞争优势。

用户界面 (UI) 设计侧重于数字产品的视觉和交互元素，例如按钮、菜单和图标。 另一方面，用户体验 (UX) 设计侧重于使用产品的整体体验，包括可用性、可访问性和情感影响。

企业如何将有效的 UI/UX 设计融入到他们的产品中？

企业可以通过确定用户需求和期望的优先级、进行用户研究和测试、与利益相关者和团队成员协作以及利用设计和原型制作工具，将有效的 UI/UX 设计融入到他们的产品中。

通过创造积极的用户体验，企业可以提高客户满意度和忠诚度，建立强大的品牌形象，并在市场上获得竞争优势。

感谢您的阅读！
Soumyadeep Mandal @imsampro Soumyadeep Mandal @imsampro



原文:

In today's digital age, user experience (UX) and user interface (UI) design have become increasingly important in the success of online businesses and platforms. UI/UX design involves creating a seamless and intuitive experience for users, ensuring that their needs and expectations are met throughout their interaction with the product. Whether it's a website, mobile application, or software, a well-executed UI/UX design can make a significant difference in user engagement, retention, and overall satisfaction. In this article, we'll explore why UI/UX design is important, how it impacts user behavior and business success, and best practices for implementing effective UI/UX design.

1. What is UI/UX Design and Why Does it Matter?

Defining UI/UX Design

UI/UX design refers to the process of creating digital interfaces that are visually appealing, user-friendly, and provide a seamless user experience. It involves designing the user interface (UI) of a website or mobile application to create a visually attractive and easy-to-navigate platform. User experience (UX) design involves making sure that the user can use the interface in a logical and intuitive way.

The Importance of UI/UX Design in the Digital Age

In today’s digital age, it is crucial to have a well-designed and user-friendly interface for your website or mobile application. With so many options available, users have become more demanding and expect a seamless user experience. UI/UX design ensures that users can navigate your website or application easily, and are more likely to stay engaged with your brand.

2. The Impact of UI/UX Design on User Engagement and Retention

Creating Positive User Experiences

UI/UX design plays a critical role in creating positive experiences for users. When a website or mobile application is well-designed and easy to use, users are more likely to stay engaged with the product, service or brand.

Reducing User Frustration and Abandonment

Poor UI/UX design can lead to frustration and abandonment by users. When a website or mobile application is difficult to navigate or confusing, users are more likely to leave and seek out another option.

Influencing User Behavior and Conversions

UI/UX design can also influence user behavior and conversions. When a website or mobile application is designed with the user in mind, it can increase the likelihood of a user taking a desired action, such as making a purchase or signing up for a service.

3. The Role of UI/UX Design in Creating a Strong Brand Identity

Establishing Brand Consistency and Recognition

UI/UX design is an important part of establishing and maintaining a strong brand identity. Consistency in design and branding across all digital platforms helps to build recognition and trust with users.

Creating Emotional Connections with Users

UI/UX design is also important for creating emotional connections with users. When a website or mobile application is designed with the user in mind, it can evoke emotions such as trust, happiness, or excitement. These emotions can help build a deeper connection with the brand.

Increasing Brand Trust and Loyalty

UI/UX design can also play a role in increasing brand trust and loyalty. When a website or mobile application is well-designed, easy to use and provides a positive user experience, users are more likely to trust the brand and remain loyal customers.

4. How UI/UX Design Can Improve Customer Satisfaction and Loyalty

Understanding User Needs and Expectations

UI/UX design involves understanding user needs and expectations. By crafting a digital interface that responds to user needs and provides a positive experience, satisfaction and loyalty can increase.

Incorporating User Feedback and Iteration

UI/UX design is an iterative process and involves incorporating user feedback into the design process to improve the user experience. By listening to user feedback, designers can make improvements to the design and improve user satisfaction.

Building Long-Term Customer Relationships

UI/UX design can be the foundation of building long-term customer relationships. When a website or mobile application provides a positive user experience, it can lead to customer loyalty and repeat business. Additionally, satisfied users are more likely to recommend the product or service to others, leading to further growth and success for the brand.

5. The Business Benefits of Investing in UI/UX Design

Whether you're a startup, small business, or large corporation, investing in UI/UX design can have a significant impact on your bottom line. Here are a few key ways that incorporating good design practices can benefit your business:

Reducing Development Costs and Time-to-Market

By incorporating UI/UX design early in the development process, you can avoid costly redesigns and rework down the road. Designers can identify potential issues or roadblocks early on and work to address them before they become major problems. This can also help reduce the time it takes to bring a product to market by allowing for more streamlined development and testing.

Increasing Revenue and Profitability

Good UI/UX design can lead to higher customer satisfaction, which in turn can lead to increased sales, repeat business, and positive word-of-mouth referrals. Design that is specifically tailored to your target audience can also help attract new customers, leading to increased revenue and profitability.

Gaining a Competitive Advantage in the Market

In today's crowded marketplace, good design can be a key differentiator between your business and your competitors. By putting the user experience at the forefront of your product or service, you can stand out in a sea of same-y offerings and establish yourself as a leader in your industry.

6. The Evolving Landscape of UI/UX Design and the Need to Keep Up

UI/UX design is a rapidly evolving field with new technologies, trends, and user behaviors emerging all the time. As a result, it's important for businesses to keep up with these changes and adapt their design strategies accordingly. Here are a few reasons why:

Emerging UI/UX Design Trends and Technologies

New tools, software, and design techniques are constantly being developed, and it's important for designers and businesses alike to stay up-to-date with these changes. Failing to do so can result in outdated, clunky designs that fail to meet the needs and expectations of users.

Adapting to Changing User Behaviors and Expectations

As technology continues to evolve, so do user behaviors and expectations. For example, younger generations may have different preferences and expectations when it comes to things like mobile design or social media integration. By keeping up with these changes, businesses can ensure that they're meeting the needs of their target audience.

Staying Ahead of the Competition

Finally, keeping up with the latest UI/UX design trends and techniques can help businesses stay ahead of the competition. By offering a better user experience than your competitors, you can establish yourself as a leader in your industry and attract more customers and clients.

7. Best Practices for Effective UI/UX Design Implementation

So, how can businesses ensure that they're implementing effective UI/UX design practices? Here are a few best practices to keep in mind:

Collaborating with Stakeholders and Team Members

Effective UI/UX design requires collaboration between designers, developers, stakeholders, and other team members. By working together, you can ensure that design decisions are informed by a variety of perspectives and that everyone is on the same page when it comes to project goals and timelines.

Conducting User Research and Testing

User research and testing are essential components of effective UI/UX design. By gathering feedback from real users, designers can identify pain points, areas for improvement, and opportunities for innovation.

Utilizing Design and Prototyping Tools

Finally, utilizing design and prototyping tools can help streamline the design process and ensure that everyone is on the same page when it comes to design decisions. From wireframing tools to prototyping software, there are a variety of tools available to help designers and businesses create effective UI/UX designs.In conclusion, UI/UX design plays a critical role in creating successful digital products and businesses. By prioritizing user needs and expectations, businesses can create positive user experiences that drive engagement, retention, and revenue. As technology continues to evolve and user behaviors change, it is essential to stay up-to-date with emerging UI/UX design trends and best practices. By incorporating effective UI/UX design into their products and platforms, businesses can create long-term customer relationships, establish strong brand identities, and gain a competitive advantage in the market.

FAQ

Why is UI/UX design important?

UI/UX design is important because it helps businesses create digital products that meet the needs and expectations of their users. By creating positive user experiences, businesses can drive engagement, retention, and revenue, as well as establish strong brand identities and gain a competitive advantage in the market.

What is the difference between UI and UX design?

User interface (UI) design focuses on the visual and interactive elements of a digital product, such as buttons, menus, and icons. User experience (UX) design, on the other hand, focuses on the overall experience of using a product, including usability, accessibility, and emotional impact.

How can businesses incorporate effective UI/UX design into their products?

Businesses can incorporate effective UI/UX design into their products by prioritizing user needs and expectations, conducting user research and testing, collaborating with stakeholders and team members, and utilizing design and prototyping tools.

How does UI/UX design impact business success?

UI/UX design impacts business success by driving user engagement, retention, and revenue. By creating positive user experiences, businesses can increase customer satisfaction and loyalty, establish strong brand identities, and gain a competitive advantage in the market.

Thank you for reading!
Soumyadeep Mandal @imsampro

Items.Title: JavaScript 对象 #10 | Javascript Object #10
Items.Description:
发表于 4 月 15 日

到目前为止，在我们的 Javascript 对象长篇文章中，我们已经了解了 Javascript 对象的创建，什么是对象属性等。但是在这篇文章中，我们将看到 Javascript 中的对象属性。这可能会让你有点困惑，因为我们已经来自对象属性，因为它是无序键和值的集合。 但这不一样，这真的很不一样。

对象属性

对象有两种类型的属性，

  1. 数据属性。
  2. 存取器属性。

数据属性

数据属性包含值的单个位置。这意味着属性的数据存储在单个属性中。
默认情况下，所有内部属性属性都设置为 true，同时创建一个对象。属性[[Value]]的默认值是undefined 。

这是对象数据属性的第一个属性，它定义了对象中的数据是否可以重新定义或者可以通过删除运算符删除。

要在对象中定义属性的特征，我们必须使用定义属性方法，* Object.defineProperty() *

注意如果您使用Object.defineProperty()定义新属性，则该属性的所有内部属性都设置为默认值false 。

让我们举个例子，

 let person = { firstName: 'Ross', lastName: 'Rachael' };

在此示例中，我们创建了一个具有两个属性的对象，现在让我们尝试删除 firstName 属性

`delete person.firstName; console.log(person.firstName);`

现在让我们使用Object.defineProperty定义一个属性，看看会发生什么，

 `let person = {}; Object.defineProperty(person, 'ssn', { configurable: false, value: '012-38-9119' }); Object.defineProperty(person, 'ssn', { configurable: true }); `
 `TypeError: Cannot redefine property: ssn`

如您所见，它会抛出错误。因为我们将内部属性配置为 false，所以一旦将可配置属性设置为false ，我们就无法重新定义该属性。

默认情况下，此属性也设置为 TRUE，这意味着您可以使用 for...in 循环遍历所有对象属性，如下所示：

 ` let person = {}; person.age = 25; person.ssn = '012-38-9119'; for (let property in person) { console.log(property); } `

输出：

让我们操纵 Ennumerable 属性，然后再试一次，

 `let person = {}; person.age = 25; person.ssn = '012-38-9119'; Object.defineProperty(person, 'ssn', { enumerable: false }); for (let prop in person) { console.log(prop); }`

输出：

如您所见，ssn 属性没有进入控制台，因为我们已将该属性的可枚举属性设置为 false。

此属性指定 weater 属性的值是否可以更改。默认情况下，此属性也设置为 true。

正如我在开头所说，数据属性中对象中的数据存储在一个位置，即[[Value]]属性。这包含属性的实际值。

就是这样。我们将在即将发布的帖子中看到有关访问器属性的信息。

So far in our long Javascript Object post,we have seen about Javascript Object creation, what is Object properties, etc. But in the post we are going to see Object properties in Javascript.This may make you little confusing,because as we have already came through Object Properties as it was the collection unordered keys and values. But this is not same,this is really different.

Object Properties

Javascript Object properties are some of the internal attributes of the Object that define the Object characteristics.They can be defined mannually.These attributes are internal and ususally they are sorrounded by two Squre brackets.We can assign the characteristic of the individual properties,that does'nt affect the other properties in the Object.

Objects have two types of Properties,

  1. Data Properties.
  2. Accessor Properties.

Data Properties

Data Properties contains the single location for the value.that means the data of the property is stored in the a single attribute.
There are four types of attributes in Data property.

  • [[Configurarable]]

  • [[Enumerable]]

  • [[Writable]]

  • [[Value]]

By default all the internal properties attributes are set to true,while creating an Object.The default value of the attribute [[Value]] is undefined.

[[Configurarable]]

This is the first attribute of the Object data property,this defines weather the data in the Object can be redefined or either can delete via Delete Operator.

To define the Charateristic of a property in an Object.we have to use define property method, *Object.defineProperty() *

This *Object.defineProperty() * accepts the three arguments, 1.The Object to define. 2.The name of the Property in the Object.

3.A property descriptor object that has four properties: configurable, enumerable, writable, and value.

Note If you Define a new Property using the Object.defineProperty() ,all the internal attributes of the property is set the default false.

Let's make this in an example,

let person = { firstName: 'Ross', lastName: 'Rachael'
};

In this example we have create an Object with two properties,Let's now try to delete the firstName property

`delete person.firstName;
console.log(person.firstName);`

Boom,the firstName property have been deleted,as you haven't assigned any internal attributes mannually so the default value of the [[Configurable]] attribute is set to true.so you can remove it via the delete operator.

Let's now define a property using the Object.defineProperty,and see what is going to happen,

`let person = {}; Object.defineProperty(person, 'ssn', { configurable: false, value: '012-38-9119'
}); Object.defineProperty(person, 'ssn', { configurable: true
});
`

`TypeError: Cannot redefine property: ssn`

As you can see,it throws an error.because the we configured the internal attributes to false,so we cannot redefine the property once we set the configurable attribute to false.

[[Enumerable]]

[[Enumerable]] defines,whether the property can be iterable or not. By default this attribute is also set TRUE,t means that you can iterate over all object properties using the for...in loop like this:

`
let person = {};
person.age = 25;
person.ssn = '012-38-9119'; for (let property in person) { console.log(property);
}
`

OUTPUT:

Let's manipulate the Ennumerable property and try this again,

`let person = {};
person.age = 25;
person.ssn = '012-38-9119'; Object.defineProperty(person, 'ssn', { enumerable: false
}); for (let prop in person) { console.log(prop);
}`

OUTPUT:

as you can see the ssn property didn't came into console as we have set the enumerable attribute of the property to false.

[[Writable]]

This attribute specifies wheater the value of the property can be changed or not.By default this attribute is also set the true.

[[Value]]

As i have told in the beginning the data in the Object in the Data properties are stored in a single location and that is the [[Value]] attribute.This contains the actual value of a property.

That's it.We will see about the Accessor Property in upcoming post.

If you are reading this Post Please Hit Up the Like,that motivates me a lot to do and learn more aboyt Javascirpt.

Many Thanks for your Morning Time,
Sam

Items.Title: 初级 Ruby 开发人员的挑战 | Challenges of a Junior Ruby Developer
Items.Description:
虽然 Ruby 开发中有很多工作机会，但对于初级开发人员来说，要踏入大门可能具有挑战性。 这给 Ruby 作为一种语言的发展带来了问题，因为许多潜在的开发人员回避这种语言，转而转向其他语言或其他提供更有利可图机会的职业。 我写这篇文章是为了推动我仍然认为是世界上最令人愉快的编程语言之一的复兴（毫无疑问，Ruby on Rails 仍然是构建快速 MVP 的最佳框架).

让我们通过显而易见的

以下是作为初级开发人员通常会遇到的一些问题。

竞争激烈的场景：科技行业竞争激烈，许多初级开发人员都在争夺相同的职位空缺。 入门级职位的数量通常有限，而且这些职位的竞争非常激烈。 这使得初级开发人员很难脱颖而出并找到工作。

高经验要求：许多公司更愿意聘请有经验的开发人员，即使是入门级职位也是如此。 初级开发人员可能很难找到适合他们经验水平的职位空缺。 他们也可能难以获得必要的经验来胜任这些职位。

需要广泛的技能：虽然 Ruby 是一种流行的编程语言，但许多公司要求开发人员除 Ruby 之外还具备广泛的技能。 初级开发人员可能需要精通其他编程语言、框架和工具，这可能会使其难以找到工作。

对于刚起步并且可能没有太多项目可以展示的初级开发人员来说，这可能很困难。 建立强大的投资组合需要时间和精力，这可能会使初级开发人员难以涉足。

内部和参考招聘：一些职位空缺没有公开发布，公司通常依靠推荐和网络来寻找合适的候选人。 刚起步的初级开发人员可能没有广泛的人脉网络，这可能会导致难以找到职位空缺并踏入职场。

雇用初级开发人员有哪些顾虑？

在招聘时，我们通常倾向于中级开发人员而不是初级开发人员。 我们的思考过程包括：

他们可以在遗留应用程序上工作吗？ 这里的代码通常很混乱，对于初级开发人员来说，在这些应用程序中修复错误和创建新功能可能会让人不知所措。

他们需要多少时间来学习新概念？ 学习通常是在工作中完成的，虽然大公司确实提供专门的学习时间，但中小型公司通常负担不起这样做的费用。

我们有时间允许他们犯错误吗？ 这就是实习（初级开发人员获得津贴或更少的津贴）仍然可能花费更高的地方，因为高级开发人员需要花时间来支持他们并指导他们克服错误。

我们能否在初始阶段为手持它们提供足够的支持？ 很多时候，公司没有这方面的预算。 我们经常进行结对编程，在这里，如果我们不需要向开发人员解释基本概念会有所帮助。

面试问题

我在采访初级开发人员时注意到的一些问题：

几乎没有测试知识/经验：通常，几乎没有测试知识（使用 RSpec 或其他框架）

臃肿的简历：简历看起来比现实更臃肿。 申请人无法解释简历中列出的技能和经验。

多说少做：在我们解决技术问题之前，信心水平通常都很高。

对 Ruby / Rails 的了解不够：申请人在技术评估回合中卡在基本任务上。

基本经验：申请人的经验并没有突出涉足太多技术复杂性（他们的知识和经验处于非常基础的水平）

可以做些什么来解决这个问题？

作为我自己的首席工程师，关于我使用的应用程序，我也列出了我自己的一些（有点雄心勃勃的）期望。

对Ruby有很好的理解

您需要对 Ruby 编程语言有扎实的了解。 这包括理解语法、控制结构、数据结构和面向对象的编程概念，如类、对象、继承和模块。 您还应该熟悉标准的 Ruby 库。

设计模式

您应该对几个关键的设计模式有很好的理解，包括策略模式、多态性、Builder、Factory 和 SOLID 原则。 这些模式提供了一个框架，用于组织和设计可维护、可扩展且易于理解的代码

您应该对 Ruby on Rails 及其组件有很好的了解，包括模型-视图-控制器 (MVC) 架构、路由、ActiveRecord、视图、帮助程序、资产以及测试框架（如 MiniTest 和 RSpec）。

您应该具有使用至少一种 SQL 数据库的经验，例如 MySQL 或 PostgreSQL。 您应该熟悉数据类型、表、主键、外键和 SQL 查询等概念。 您还应该熟悉 MySQL Workbench 和 pgAdmin 等用于管理和查询数据库的工具。

活动记录

您应该对 ActiveRecord 及其功能有很好的理解，包括模型、关联、验证、回调、范围和迁移。

休息API

您应该熟悉 RESTful API 设计，包括 HTTP 方法、URI 设计、请求和响应格式、身份验证和安全性。

现在这在很大程度上取决于你所追求的角色，但对于 Rails 通常（除非你为一个大实体工作），你应该是一个全栈开发人员，因此一些前端和 Javascript 知识是必要的。 您应该熟悉 DOM 操作、事件处理、AJAX 和 ES6 语法等概念。

混帐

您应该熟悉 Git 及其命令，包括 git init、git add、git commit、git push、git pull 和 git merge。 您还应该熟悉常见的 Git 工作流程，例如分支、合并和变基。 还了解如何创建和审查拉取请求。 通常，这是学习和改进代码的一个很好的来源。

码头工人

随着 Devops 的理念在中小型团队中变得越来越强大，通常期望 Ruby 开发人员具备一些 Docker 知识。 作为初级 Ruby 开发人员，您应该有一些使用 Docker 及其概念的经验，包括容器、图像、Dockerfile 和 Docker Compose。 您还应该熟悉用于构建和部署应用程序的常用 Docker 命令和工作流。

软技能和其他东西

你不需要成为演说家，但你应该能够足够好地沟通（你的听力很重要）。 去掉你简历上所有的废话，只谈论你真正知道的。 公司确实有文化轮，在这里你应该让你作为团队合作者的个性闪耀。 开始学习 Leetcode、Hackerrank 等，以快速掌握算法知识。 虽然这并不总是一项要求，但许多公司仍然会通过向您发送一些要解决的算法问题来开始他们的面试过程。 其他人可能对您解决问题的能力感兴趣，而不是实际期望您解决算法，并使用白板方法（如果在本地完成）或一些远程编码方法（如果远程完成）。 在这里，他们希望了解您提出的问题以及您采取的方法。

这一切都值得吗？

现在，虽然这似乎是一项艰巨的任务，但请相信我——这是值得的。 您现在不仅要努力在竞争中脱颖而出，还要努力成为一名中级开发人员。 当你加入时，你可能还会处理遗留的应用程序，这些应用程序可能会让人不知所措，在公司内部有人指导你肯定会有所帮助，并在你了解这些应用程序如何工作的初始阶段牵着你的手。

通过建立强大的投资组合、学习额外的技能、建立人脉、申请实习和坚持不懈，初级开发人员可以增加在这个令人兴奋的领域找到工作的机会。 我个人正在尝试找出可以为初级开发人员提供机会的想法，并使他们能够更轻松地获得机会。



原文:

While there are many job opportunities in Ruby development, it can be challenging for junior developers to get their foot in the door. This is causing a problem for the growth of Ruby as a language, as many potential developers shy away from the language, and move to some other language or some other career which offer more lucrative opportunities. I am writing this article in my effort to try to push for the revival of what I still consider one of the most enjoyable programming languages to work with in the world (hands down Ruby on Rails remains the best framework even today to build an quick MVP).

Let's go through the obvious

Here are some issues that you will commonly face as a junior developer.

Highly competitive scene: The tech industry is highly competitive, and many junior developers are vying for the same job openings. There are often a limited number of entry-level positions available, and the competition for these roles can be fierce. This makes it difficult for junior developers to stand out and land a job.

High experience requirement: Many companies prefer to hire developers with experience, even for entry-level positions. Junior developers may struggle to find job openings that are suitable for their level of experience. They may also struggle to gain the necessary experience to qualify for these positions.

Wide skillset required: While Ruby is a popular programming language, many companies require developers to have a wide range of skills in addition to Ruby. Junior developers may need to be proficient in other programming languages, frameworks, and tools, which can make it difficult to land a job.

Strong portfolio expectations: In addition to experience and skills, many companies also require junior developers to have a strong portfolio of work. This can be difficult for junior developers who are just starting out and may not have many projects to showcase. Building a strong portfolio can take time and effort, which can make it difficult for junior developers to get their foot in the door.

Internal and reference hires: Some job openings are not publicly advertised, and companies often rely on referrals and networking to find suitable candidates. Junior developers who are just starting out may not have an extensive network of contacts, which can make it difficult to find job openings and get their foot in the door.

What are the concerns of hiring a junior developer?

When we are hiring, we often lean towards a mid-level developer over a junior developer. Our thought process includes:

Can they work on legacy applications? The code here is often messy, and for a junior developer, fixing bugs and creating new features in these applications can be overwhelming.

How much time do they need to learn newer concepts? Learning is generally done on the job, and while bigger companies do provide dedicated time for learning, smaller and medium-sized companies cannot generally afford to do this.

Do we have the luxury of time to allow them to make mistakes? This is where internships (where junior developers are being paid a stipend or less) can still come at a higher cost, as senior developers would need to dedicate time to supporting them and guiding them through their mistakes.

Can we provide enough support for hand-holding them through the initial phase? Quite often, companies don't have a budget for this. We do pair-programming quite often, and here, it would help if we don't need to explain basic concepts to the developer.

Interview issues

Some issues that I have noticed during our interviews with junior developers:

Little to no testing knowledge / experience: Often, there is little to no knowledge of testing (using a framework like RSpec or otherwise)

Bloated CVs: The resume seems more bloated than reality. Applicants are not able to explain the skills and experience listed in their resume.

More talk and less action: Confidence levels are generally quite high until we get to technical questions.

Not enough knowledge of Ruby / Rails: Applicants get stuck on basic tasks during a technical assessment round.

Basic experience: Applicant experience doesn't highlight dabbling with much technical complexity (their knowledge and experience is on a very basic level)

What can be done to fix this?

Being a Lead Engineer myself, in regard to the apps I work with, I've also listed some of my own (slightly ambitious) expectations.

Good understanding of Ruby

You will need to have a solid understanding of the Ruby programming language. This includes understanding the syntax, control structures, data structures, and object-oriented programming concepts like classes, objects, inheritance, and modules. You should also be familiar with the standard Ruby library.

Design patterns

You should have a good understanding of several key design patterns, including the Strategy pattern, Polymorphism, Builder, Factory, and SOLID principles. These patterns provide a framework for organizing and designing code that is maintainable, scalable, and easy to understand

Ruby on Rails

You should have a good understanding of Ruby on Rails and its components, including the Model-View-Controller (MVC) architecture, routing, ActiveRecord, views, helpers, assets, and testing frameworks like MiniTest and RSpec.

SQL Databases

You should have experience working with at least one SQL database, such as MySQL or PostgreSQL. You should be familiar with concepts like data types, tables, primary keys, foreign keys, and SQL queries. You should also be familiar with tools like MySQL Workbench and pgAdmin for managing and querying databases.

ActiveRecord

You should have a good understanding of ActiveRecord and its features, including models, associations, validations, callbacks, scopes, and migrations.

REST API

You should be familiar with RESTful API design, including HTTP methods, URI design, request and response formats, authentication, and security.

Javascript

Now this would largely depend on the role you are pursuing, but quite often with Rails (unless you are working for a big entity), you are expected to be a fullstack developer, and therefore some frontend and Javascript knowledge is necessary. JavaScript is a programming language used to add interactivity and dynamic behavior to web applications. You should be familiar with concepts like DOM manipulation, event handling, AJAX, and ES6 syntax.

Git

You should be familiar with Git and its commands, including git init, git add, git commit, git push, git pull, and git merge. You should also be familiar with common Git workflows like branching, merging, and rebasing. Also understand how to create and review pull requests. Quite often, this is a very good source of learning and improving your code.

Docker

With the philosophy of Devops geting stronger in smaller and medium-sized teams, knowledge of some Docker is generally expected from a Ruby Developer. Docker is a platform used to build, ship, and run applications in containers. As a junior Ruby developer, you should have some experience working with Docker and its concepts, including containers, images, Dockerfiles, and Docker Compose. You should also be familiar with common Docker commands and workflows for building and deploying applications.

Soft-skills and other stuff

You don't need to be an orator, but you should be able to communicate well enough (your listening skills are very important). Get rid of all the fluff on your resume, and talk only about what you actually know. Companies do have culture rounds, and here you should let your personality as a team player shine. Get cracking on Leetcode, Hackerrank, etc. to get your knowledge of algorithms up to speed. While this is not always a requirement, many companies still commence their interview process by sending you some algorithmic problems to solve. Others might be interested in your problem solving skills rather than actually expecting you to solve the algorithms, and use the white-board method (if done locally) or some remote coding method if done remotely. Here, they would like at the questions you ask, and the approach you take.

Is all of this worth it?

Now, while this might seem like a tall order, trust me - it's worth it. You are now trying not only to get ahead of your competition, but are also working your way into becoming a mid-level developer. When you do join, you might also work on legacy applications that can be overwhelming, and it would definitely help to have someone mentor you inside your company, and hold your hand through the initial period where you get an understanding of how these apps work.

By building a strong portfolio, learning additional skills, networking, applying for internships, and being persistent, junior developers can increase their chances of landing a job in this exciting field. I am personally trying to figure out ideas that can provide opportunities for junior developers myself and get them to a point where they can get access to opportunities more easily.

Items.Title: Blazor WebAssembly 与 Blazor Server：您应该选择哪一个？ | Blazor WebAssembly vs. Blazor Server: Which One Should You Choose?
Items.Description:
虽然这两种模型共享相同的编程模型，但它们之间存在显着差异。 在本文中，我们将探讨 Blazor WebAssembly 和 Blazor Server 之间的区别，并帮助您确定哪一个适合您的项目。

当用户请求应用程序时，应用程序的代码被下载到客户端的机器上，在浏览器的沙箱中编译和执行。 这意味着该应用程序可以离线运行，并且用户即使没有连接到互联网也可以继续使用该应用程序。

另一方面，Blazor Server 在服务器上运行应用程序并使用 SignalR 提供客户端和服务器之间的实时通信。 当用户请求应用程序时，服务器将 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 发送到客户端。 用户与应用程序交互，应用程序将用户的输入发送到服务器进行处理。 服务器然后将更新后的 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 发送给客户端，然后重复该过程。

你应该选择哪一个？
如果您的应用程序需要离线运行，或者您需要利用客户端处理能力，那么 Blazor WebAssembly 是更好的选择。 另一方面，如果您的应用程序需要实时通信，或者您有性能方面的顾虑，那么 Blazor Server 是最佳选择。

在本文中，我们探讨了 Blazor WebAssembly 和 Blazor Server 之间的区别，并帮助您确定哪一个适合您的项目。 请记住，这两种模型共享相同的编程模型，因此无论您选择哪一种，您仍然能够利用 C# 和 .NET 的强大功能来构建您的 Web 应用程序。

原文:

Blazor, the open-source web framework from Microsoft, allows developers to build web applications using C# and .NET instead of JavaScript. Blazor supports two deployment models: WebAssembly and Server. While both models share the same programming model, there are significant differences between them. In this post, we'll explore the differences between Blazor WebAssembly and Blazor Server and help you decide which one is right for your project.

Blazor WebAssembly
Blazor WebAssembly allows you to run the entire application in the client's web browser. When the user requests the application, the application's code is downloaded to the client's machine, compiled, and executed inside the browser's sandbox. This means that the application can run offline, and the user can continue to use the app even when they're not connected to the internet.

Blazor Server
Blazor Server, on the other hand, runs the application on the server and uses SignalR to provide real-time communication between the client and the server. When the user requests the application, the server sends the HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to the client. The user interacts with the app, and the app sends the user's input to the server for processing. The server then sends the updated HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to the client, and the process repeats.

Which One Should You Choose?
The choice between Blazor WebAssembly and Blazor Server depends on your project's requirements. If your application needs to run offline, or you need to take advantage of client-side processing power, then Blazor WebAssembly is the better option. On the other hand, if your application requires real-time communication, or you have performance concerns, then Blazor Server is the way to go.

In this post, we explored the differences between Blazor WebAssembly and Blazor Server and helped you decide which one is right for your project. Remember, both models share the same programming model, so whichever one you choose, you'll still be able to leverage the power of C# and .NET to build your web application.

Items.Title: 揭秘 JavaScript 中的 Promises：完整指南 | Demystifying Promises in JavaScript: A Complete Guide
Items.Description:
发表于 4 月 15 日

简介：
异步编程是 JavaScript 中的一个基本概念，允许开发人员编写可以同时处理多个任务的非阻塞代码。 但是，promises 有时会让刚接触 JavaScript 或异步编程的开发人员感到困惑。 在这份综合指南中，我们将揭开 JavaScript 中承诺的神秘面纱，解释它们是什么、它们如何工作以及如何在您的代码中有效地使用它们。

第 1 节：理解 Promises在本节中，我们将提供 promises 的清晰定义并解释它们在 JavaScript 中的用途。 我们将介绍承诺的基本语法，包括如何创建、解决和拒绝承诺。 我们还将讨论 promises 的三种状态——pending、fulfilled 和 rejected——以及如何使用 catch 块处理 promises 中的错误。

 const promise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => { setTimeout(() => { resolve("Promise resolved!"); }, 1000); }); promise.then((result) => { console.log(result); // Output: Promise resolved! }).catch((error) => { console.error(error); });

第 2 节：链接承诺在本节中，我们将探索如何链接承诺，这是允许顺序执行异步操作的承诺的强大功能。 我们将介绍如何使用 .then() 方法来链接承诺，以及如何在承诺链之间传递数据。 我们还将讨论承诺组合的概念，其中可以并行或顺序组合和解决承诺。

 const fetchUserData = () => { return new Promise((resolve) => { setTimeout(() => { resolve({ id: 1, name: "John", age: 30 }); }, 1000); }); }; const fetchUserDetails = (userId) => { return new Promise((resolve) => { setTimeout(() => { resolve(`User ID: ${userId}, Name: John, Age: 30`); }, 1000); }); }; fetchUserData() .then((user) => fetchUserDetails(user.id)) .then((userDetails) => { console.log(userDetails); // Output: User ID: 1, Name: John, Age: 30 }) .catch((error) => { console.error(error); });

第 3 节：使用多个 Promise在本节中，我们将深入研究使用 Promise.all() 和 Promise.race() 方法同时处理多个 Promise。 我们将解释如何使用 Promise.all() 等待一系列承诺的解决，以及如何使用 Promise.race() 获得第一个解决承诺的结果。 我们还将介绍并发承诺中的错误处理和管理多个承诺的最佳实践。

 const fetchData = (url) => { return new Promise((resolve) => { setTimeout(() => { resolve(`Data from ${url}`); }, Math.random() * 2000); }); }; const urls = ["https://api.example.com/data1", "https://api.example.com/data2", "https://api.example.com/data3"]; const promises = urls.map((url) => fetchData(url)); Promise.all(promises) .then((results) => { console.log(results); // Output: ["Data from https://api.example.com/data1", "Data from https://api.example.com/data2", "Data from https://api.example.com/data3"] }) .catch((error) => { console.error(error); });

第 4 节：高级 Promise 概念在本节中，我们将介绍与 Promise 相关的高级概念，包括 async/await，它提供了一种使用 Promise 编写异步代码的更简洁的方法。 我们还将讨论 Promise.resolve() 和 Promise.reject() 方法、promise 超时以及如何处理 promise 中的异常。 我们将为这些高级概念提供实际示例和用例。

 const fetchUserData = () => { return new Promise((resolve) => { setTimeout(() => { resolve({ id: 1, name: "John", age: 30 }); }, 1000); }); }; const fetchUserDetails = (userId) => { return new Promise((resolve) => { setTimeout(() => { resolve(`User ID: ${userId}, Name: John, Age: 30`); }, 1000); }); }; const getUserDetails = async () => { try { const user = await fetchUserData(); const userDetails = await fetchUserDetails(user.id); console.log(userDetails); // Output: User ID: 1, Name: John, Age: 30 } catch (error) { console.error(error); } }; getUserDetails();

第 5 部分：Promises 最佳实践
在本节中，我们将提供在 JavaScript 代码中有效使用 promises 的最佳实践。 我们将讨论错误处理、错误传播、处理未处理的承诺、避免承诺反模式以及优化承诺性能。 我们还将提供调试和测试承诺的技巧。

结论：
在这本综合指南中，我们揭开了 JavaScript 中承诺的神秘面纱，涵盖了它们的基本语法、链接、处理多个承诺、高级概念和最佳实践。 通过遵循本指南中提供的最佳实践和技巧，您可以利用承诺来编写更健壮和可维护的 JavaScript 应用程序。 一旦被暂停，在取消暂停之前，eckdev 将无法发表评论或发布帖子。

原文:

Eyüp Can Kayadarçin

Posted on Apr 15

   

Intro:
Asynchronous programming is a fundamental concept in JavaScript, allowing developers to write non-blocking code that can handle multiple tasks concurrently. Promises are a key feature in modern JavaScript for managing asynchronous operations, providing a more elegant and readable way to handle async code compared to traditional callbacks. However, promises can sometimes be confusing for developers who are new to JavaScript or asynchronous programming. In this comprehensive guide, we will demystify promises in JavaScript, explaining what they are, how they work, and how to use them effectively in your code.

Section 1: Understanding Promises In this section, we will provide a clear definition of promises and explain their purpose in JavaScript. We will cover the basic syntax of promises, including how to create, resolve, and reject promises. We will also discuss the three states of promises - pending, fulfilled, and rejected - and how to handle errors in promises using catch blocks.

const promise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
  setTimeout(() => {
    resolve("Promise resolved!");
  }, 1000);
});

promise.then((result) => {
  console.log(result); // Output: Promise resolved!
}).catch((error) => {
  console.error(error);
});

Section 2: Chaining Promises In this section, we will explore how to chain promises, which is a powerful feature of promises that allows for sequential execution of async operations. We will cover how to use the .then() method to chain promises, and how to pass data between promise chains. We will also discuss the concept of promise composition, where promises can be combined and resolved in parallel or sequentially.

const fetchUserData = () => {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      resolve({ id: 1, name: "John", age: 30 });
    }, 1000);
  });
};

const fetchUserDetails = (userId) => {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      resolve(`User ID: ${userId}, Name: John, Age: 30`);
    }, 1000);
  });
};

fetchUserData()
  .then((user) => fetchUserDetails(user.id))
  .then((userDetails) => {
    console.log(userDetails); // Output: User ID: 1, Name: John, Age: 30
  })
  .catch((error) => {
    console.error(error);
  });

Section 3: Working with Multiple Promises In this section, we will dive into handling multiple promises concurrently using Promise.all() and Promise.race() methods. We will explain how to use Promise.all() to wait for an array of promises to resolve, and how to use Promise.race() to get the result of the first resolved promise. We will also cover error handling in concurrent promises and best practices for managing multiple promises.

const fetchData = (url) => {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      resolve(`Data from ${url}`);
    }, Math.random() * 2000);
  });
};

const urls = ["https://api.example.com/data1", "https://api.example.com/data2", "https://api.example.com/data3"];

const promises = urls.map((url) => fetchData(url));

Promise.all(promises)
  .then((results) => {
    console.log(results); // Output: ["Data from https://api.example.com/data1", "Data from https://api.example.com/data2", "Data from https://api.example.com/data3"]
  })
  .catch((error) => {
    console.error(error);
  });

Section 4: Advanced Promise Concepts In this section, we will cover advanced concepts related to promises, including async/await, which provides a more concise way to write async code using promises. We will also discuss Promise.resolve() and Promise.reject() methods, promise timeouts, and how to handle exceptions in promises. We will provide practical examples and use cases for these advanced concepts.

const fetchUserData = () => {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      resolve({ id: 1, name: "John", age: 30 });
    }, 1000);
  });
};

const fetchUserDetails = (userId) => {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      resolve(`User ID: ${userId}, Name: John, Age: 30`);
    }, 1000);
  });
};

const getUserDetails = async () => {
  try {
    const user = await fetchUserData();
    const userDetails = await fetchUserDetails(user.id);
    console.log(userDetails); // Output: User ID: 1, Name: John, Age: 30
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
  }
};

getUserDetails();

Section 5: Promises Best Practices
In this section, we will provide best practices for using promises effectively in your JavaScript code. We will discuss error handling, error propagation, handling unhandled promises, avoiding promise anti-patterns, and optimizing promise performance. We will also provide tips for debugging and testing promises.

Conclusion:
In this comprehensive guide, we have demystified promises in JavaScript, covering their basic syntax, chaining, handling multiple promises, advanced concepts, and best practices. Promises are a powerful tool for managing asynchronous operations in JavaScript, and understanding how they work is essential for writing clean and efficient async code. By following the best practices and tips provided in this guide, you can leverage promises to write more robust and maintainable JavaScript applications. | EckDev

Items.Description:
发表于 4 月 15 日

欢迎回来！ 在上一篇文章中，我们了解了对象属性及其一个属性数据属性。现在我们将在这篇文章中了解访问器属性。

与数据属性一样，访问器属性具有 [[Configurable]] 和 [[Enumerable]] 属性。但是访问器属性具有 [[Get]] 和 [[Set]] 属性，而不是 [[Value]] 和 [[Writable] ]].

当你想从访问器属性中读取数据时，[[Get]] 方法会自动调用以返回一个值。当你想为属性赋值时，[[Set]] 函数会自动调用。

要定义访问器属性，您需要使用 Object.defineProperty() 方法。 或示例：

 `let person = { firstName: 'John', lastName: 'Doe' } Object.defineProperty(person, 'fullName', { get: function () { return this.firstName + ' ' + this.lastName; }, set: function (value) { let parts = value.split(' '); if (parts.length == 2) { this.firstName = parts[0]; this.lastName = parts[1]; } else { throw 'Invalid name format'; } } }); console.log(person.fullName);`

输出

在此示例中，Person 对象包含两个属性，firstName 和 lastName。然后我们将 fullName 属性作为访问器属性添加到 Person 对象。在 fullName 访问器属性中，

  • 如果全名的格式不正确，即名字、空格和姓氏，它将抛出错误。

从 ES5 开始，我们可以使用 Object.defineProperties() 方法在单个语句中定义多个属性。

 `var product = {}; Object.defineProperties(product, { name: { value: 'Smartphone' }, price: { value: 799 }, tax: { value: 0.1 }, netPrice: { get: function () { return this.price * (1 + this.tax); } } }); console.log('The net price of a ' + product.name + ' is ' + product.netPrice.toFixed(2) + ' USD');`

输出

`The net price of a Smartphone is 878.90 USD`

在此示例中，我们定义了三个数据属性：name、price 和 tax，以及一个用于产品对象的访问器属性 netPrice。

在 Javascript 中我们还可以使用 Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor() 方法找到对象的属性描述符，Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor() 方法有两个参数：

  1. 一个东西。
  2. 对象的属性。

它返回描述属性的描述符对象。 描述符对象有四个属性：可配置、可枚举、可写和值。

 `let person = { firstName: 'John', lastName: 'Doe' }; let descriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(person, 'firstName'); console.log(descriptor);`

输出

`{ value: 'John', writable: true, enumerable: true, configurable: true }`

这就是关于 Javascript 对象属性的全部内容。我们将在下一篇博文中看到更多关于对象的有趣内容。

如果您正在阅读这篇文章，并且发现有帮助。请给我一个赞和评论，这将激励我很多并用 Javascript 做更多有趣的事情。

谢谢你的平均时间，
山姆

Welcome back! in the last post we have seen about the Object properties and it one property Data property.Now we are going to see about the accessor property in this Post.

As like Data Properties,accessor property have [[Configurable]] and [[Enumerable]] attributes.But the accessor property have the [[Get]] and [[Set]] attributes instead of [[Value]] and [[Writable]].

When you want to read a data from the accessor property the [[Get]] method is automatically called to return a value.when you want to assign a value to the property [[Set]] function is called automatically.

To define a accessor property,you need to use Object.defineProperty() method. or example:

`let person = { firstName: 'John', lastName: 'Doe'
} Object.defineProperty(person, 'fullName', { get: function () { return this.firstName + ' ' + this.lastName; }, set: function (value) { let parts = value.split(' '); if (parts.length == 2) { this.firstName = parts[0]; this.lastName = parts[1]; } else { throw 'Invalid name format'; } }
}); console.log(person.fullName);`

OUTPUT

in this example,the Person Object conatins two properties,firstName and lastName.Then we are adding the fullName property to the Person Oject as an accessor property.In the fullName accessor property,

  • The [[Get]] returns the full name that is the result of concatenating of firstName, space, and lastName.

  • The [[Set]] method splits the argument by the space and assigns the firstName and lastName properties the corresponding parts of the name.

  • If the full name is not in the correct format i.e., first name, space, and last name, it will throw an error.

From the ES5,we can define a multiple property in a single statement using the Object.defineProperties() method.

`var product = {}; Object.defineProperties(product, { name: { value: 'Smartphone' }, price: { value: 799 }, tax: { value: 0.1 }, netPrice: { get: function () { return this.price * (1 + this.tax); } }
}); console.log('The net price of a ' + product.name + ' is ' + product.netPrice.toFixed(2) + ' USD');`

OUTPUT

`The net price of a Smartphone is 878.90 USD`

In this example, we defined three data properties: name, price, and tax, and one accessor property netPrice for the product object.

In Javascript we can also find the property descriptor of an Object using the Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor() method,The Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor() method takes two arguments:

  1. An Object.
  2. A Property of an Object.

It returns a descriptor object that describes a property. The descriptor object has four properties: configurable, enumerable, writable, and value.

`let person = { firstName: 'John', lastName: 'Doe'
}; let descriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(person, 'firstName'); console.log(descriptor);`

OUTPUT

`{ value: 'John', writable: true, enumerable: true, configurable: true }`

That's all about the Javascript Object Properties.We will see more intersting things about Object in Upcoming Post.

If you are reading this post,and find helpfull.Please give a like and comments that will motivate me a lot and do more interesting things in Javascript.

Thanks for your mean Time,
Sam

Items.Description:
管理旅馆可能是一项艰巨的任务，尤其是当您手动进行管理时。 从跟踪客房预订、办理入住和退房手续、管理家政和维护以及处理付款，它很快就会变得不堪重负。 然而，在旅馆管理系统的帮助下，这些任务可以轻松管理，让您专注于为客人提供难忘的体验。

什么是宿舍管理系统？

宿舍管理系统是旨在简化宿舍管理的软件。 它使管理预订、房间分配、入住和退房的过程自动化。 此外，它还可以帮助管理和维护、库存管理以及计费和付款。 在宿舍管理系统的帮助下，宿舍经理可以从一个平台管理所有这些任务。

使用宿舍管理系统的好处

高效的预订管理：

使用宿舍管理软件，您可以有效地管理您的预订。 您可以轻松查看所有预订、管理房间分配和跟踪入住率。 这有助于避免超额预订和重复预订，从而导致客人不满意。

实时可用性：

宿舍管理系统可让您查看实时房间可用性，从而轻松管理房间分配并避免混乱。 您还可以设置自动电子邮件通知，让客人了解他们的预订和任何更改。

简化的入住和退房：

宿舍管理系统简化了入住和退房流程。 客人可以快速轻松地办理入住手续，他们的详细信息存储在系统中以备将来参考。 该系统还可以自动计算和处理账单，节省时间并降低出错风险。

改进的内务管理和维护：

宿舍管理系统可以帮助改善客房服务和维护。 您可以安排清洁和维护任务，跟踪它们的进度，并将任务分配给员工。 这有助于确保您的旅馆始终保持清洁和维护良好，这对于客人的满意度至关重要。

库存管理：

宿舍管理系统可以帮助您有效地管理您的库存。 您可以跟踪毛巾、床上用品和洗浴用品等便利设施的可用性，确保您始终有足够的用品。 这有助于避免必需品用完，从而导致客人不满意。

计费和付款管理：

使用宿舍管理系统，您可以轻松管理账单和付款。 该系统可以生成发票、处理付款并跟踪未结余额。 这有助于降低出错风险并确保快速有效地处理付款。

选择合适的宿舍管理系统

选择宿舍管理系统时，需要考虑几个因素。 首先，您需要考虑对您的旅馆很重要的功能。

例如，如果您有一个有很多房间的大型旅馆，您可能需要一个能够处理大量预订的系统。 此外，您需要考虑供应商提供的易用性和支持级别。

实施宿舍管理系统

实施宿舍管理系统是一个简单的过程。 首先，您需要选择供应商并选择符合您要求的软件包。

接下来，您需要设置系统，包括添加您的客房库存、价格和空房情况。 您还需要培训您的员工如何有效地使用该系统。

结论

宿舍管理系统是一个强大的工具，可以帮助您轻松管理宿舍。 它可以自动执行与旅馆管理相关的许多任务，包括预订、入住和退房、家政和维护、库存管理以及计费和付款。 通过旅舍管理系统，您可以为客人提供难忘的住宿体验

Managing a hostel can be a daunting task, especially if you are doing it manually. From keeping track of room reservations, handling check-ins and check-outs, managing housekeeping and maintenance, and handling payments, it can quickly become overwhelming. However, with the help of a hostel management system, these tasks can be managed with ease, allowing you to focus on providing a memorable experience for your guests.

What is a Hostel Management System?

A hostel management system is software designed to streamline the management of hostels. It automates the process of managing reservations, room assignments, check-ins, and check-outs. Additionally, it can help with housekeeping and maintenance, inventory management, and billing and payments. With the help of a hostel management system, hostel managers can manage all of these tasks from a single platform.

Benefits of Using a Hostel Management System

Efficient Management of Bookings:

With a hostel management software, you can manage your bookings efficiently. You can easily view all your reservations, manage room allocations and track occupancy levels. This helps to avoid overbooking and double-booking, which can lead to guest dissatisfaction.

Real-Time Availability:

A hostel management system allows you to see real-time room availability, making it easy to manage room assignments and avoid confusion. You can also set up automatic email notifications to keep guests informed about their reservations and any changes.

Streamlined Check-In and Check-Out:

A hostel management system simplifies the check-in and check-out process. Guests can check-in quickly and easily, and their details are stored in the system for future reference. The system can also automatically calculate and process bills, saving time and reducing the risk of errors.

Improved Housekeeping and Maintenance:

A hostel management system can help improve housekeeping and maintenance. You can schedule cleaning and maintenance tasks, track their progress, and assign tasks to staff members. This helps to ensure that your hostel is always clean and well-maintained, which is essential for guest satisfaction.

Inventory Management:

A hostel management system can help you manage your inventory efficiently. You can track the availability of amenities such as towels, bedding, and toiletries, ensuring that you always have enough supplies. This helps to avoid running out of essential items, which can lead to guest dissatisfaction.

Billing and Payment Management:

With a hostel management system, you can manage billing and payments easily. The system can generate invoices, process payments, and keep track of outstanding balances. This helps to reduce the risk of errors and ensures that payments are processed quickly and efficiently.

Choosing the Right Hostel Management System

When choosing a hostel management system, there are several factors to consider. Firstly, you need to consider the features that are important to your hostel.

For example, if you have a large hostel with many rooms, you may need a system that can handle a high volume of reservations and bookings. Additionally, you need to consider the ease of use and the level of support provided by the vendor.

Implementing a Hostel Management System

Implementing a hostel management system is a straightforward process. Firstly, you need to choose a vendor and select the package that meets your requirements.

Next, you need to set up the system, including adding your room inventory, pricing, and availability. You also need to train your staff on how to use the system effectively.

Conclusion

A hostel management system is a powerful tool that can help you manage your hostel with ease. It automates many of the tasks associated with hostel management, including reservations, check-ins, and check-outs, housekeeping and maintenance, inventory management, and billing and payments. With a hostel management system, you can provide your guests with a memorable
